summary: Exported Windows binaries can't have their "Licence" field customized, so it's always "MIT" like Godot

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested versions
v4.4.1.stable.official

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Mobile) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (Intel Corporation; 32.0.101.6129) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1185G7 @ 3.00GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

I happened to come across this when exporting to a .NET Godot game for Windows64 AOT (with dotnet/embed_build_outputs=true), but it probably happens elsewhere too. If you analyse the exported executable, it contains a version.txt file with info that is a mix between the game and the game engine itself, leading all games to look like they're under the MIT license :)

Here's a look at the resulting version.txt if you don't set any of the Application fields in the export window:

VALUE "CompanyName",       "Godot Engine"
VALUE "FileDescription",   "Godot Engine"
VALUE "FileVersion",       "1.0.0.0"
VALUE "ProductName",       "Godot Engine"
VALUE "Licence",           "MIT"
VALUE "LegalCopyright",    "(c) 2007-present Juan Linietsky, Ariel Manzur and Godot Engine contributors"
VALUE "Info",              "https://godotengine.org"
VALUE "ProductVersion",    "1.0.0.0"

If you do update the export settings to match the game name, company name etc. it comes out as this:

VALUE "CompanyName",       "My Company"
VALUE "FileDescription",   "My Game Name"
VALUE "FileVersion",       "1.0.0.0"
VALUE "ProductName",       "My Game Name"
VALUE "Licence",           "MIT"
VALUE "LegalCopyright",    "(c) My Company"
VALUE "Info",              "https://godotengine.org"
VALUE "ProductVersion",    "1.0.0.0"

Since there is no way to set the License or the Info, the resulting version.txt comes out as a mix between the game itself and the game engine.

### Steps to reproduce

Export a game .NET game using native AOT for Windows64. On Windows you can right click the resulting .exe file, extract it with something like 7-zip. Then go into the .rsrc folder and open the version.txt file

summary: The shader cache is in the iOS documents folder

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.4 iOS

### System information

iOS

### Issue description

In Godot 4.4 on iOS my app will not pass app review if it would be publishing the documents directory to Files/Local Storage because the Godot shader cache is in it instead of being in the Application Support directory or cache directory where it belongs.

I’d recommend placing the shader cache in the Application Support directory if the reasoning was avoiding system cache purges.

I haven't checked, but if Godot does put the files in the Application Support directory conditionally in production then a readme.txt should be placed next to shader_cache with information for developers deploying in XCode and TestFlight environments.

### Steps to reproduce

In the iOS export dialog add 
```xml
<key>LSSupportsOpeningDocumentsInPlace</key>
<true/>
<key>UIFileSharingEnabled</key>
<true/>
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Export to iOS, install using Xcode

### Issue description

There are two primary ways to create new files in Godot:

1) Right click on a folder/file in the file system dock, and use the 'Create New' submenu:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3257cc57-8c5f-4e51-a059-5893593bc12c)

---

The `ContextMenuSlot::CONTEXT_SLOT_FILESYSTEM_CREATE` only adds a member into the *first* slot. The second slot doesn't appear to be data driven at all. I would like to see these two menus "linked" so that changes to one effects changes to the other. 

Note: One difference between these two menus is the inclusion of the 'New' keyword in the second one. I recall some discussion about this exact topic, actually, but I cannot find the issue. 


### Steps to reproduce

Open the MRP. You may need to enable the 'Create Actor' plugin. Attempt to create an actor using both workflows shown above. Only the 'Create New' menu shows the new 'Create Actor' option.

description.text:
### Tested versions

### System information

w10 64

### Issue description

Watch the video. When you click on a node, it takes some time for the node to appear as selected, even though it is already selected in reality.

I noticed that if you move the node, it lags behind the cursor, and the node is positioned with a delay.

If you wait for the node to be fully selected, it then correctly positions itself at the cursor’s location.

My report is asking if it’s possible to eliminate or optimize the selection delay that must be waited for before the node positions correctly at the cursor.

I’m not sure if this is a bug or what exactly is happening.
It seems to be related to the complexity of the script assigned to the node. Nodes with scripts that have little code are selected faster.
As you can see in my video showing Godot 4, this problem doesn't happen, but as I mentioned earlier, it's probably because the node that moves in Godot 4 doesn't have a complex script.

summary: Move MovieWriterMJPEG class to `jpg` module it depends on

description.text:
- Fixes #90932.
- Supersedes #100166.
- ~Draft as it includes #104347 to avoid conflicts, I'll rebase once it's merged.~

---

I don't intend to work on it further, but there are various UX issues I noticed which may be worth addressing (@Calinou):

- With this PR when using `.avi` extension with the jpg module disabled, and likely already before when using any other extension than `.avi` or `.png`, the movie writer doesn't give any warning and starts, but prints an error:
```
ERROR: Can't find movie writer for file type, aborting: res://test.avi
   at: setup2 (main/main.cpp:3575)
```
It should probably validate that the file type is supported *before* running the project in movie writer mode, showing explanations to the user with a popup.

- When no movie writer write path is configured, the following dialog is shown. It has multiple UX issues.
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f475091a-e37b-4931-8809-d54a27381f70)
  * The text alignment is weird with the manual wrap on the second-to-last line.
  * It suggests two options to set a movie path but not the main one, which is the `editor/movie_writer/movie_file` project setting.
  * For a feature that has such a prominent button in the run bar, the first time experience is quite bad when you get this dialog. Similar to how we improved selecting a main scene when running for the first time, this dialog should likely give an easy option to set a default movie path project setting (e.g. derived from the project name, writing to `res://`).

- The expectation to select the output format (avi or png) just based on the file extension, with the only information available in the documentation for the project setting, is suboptimal. In the future, such features might be better served with a dedicated dialog (similar to the "Customize Run Instances..." dialog) that lets users configure all the settings for encoding.

summary: GraphNode focus navigation “down” jumps to slots (creating white rectangles) before moving to the node below

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot 4.5dev3, Godot v4.5.dev4 and also on newest Godot v4.5.dev5
- not reproducible in in Godot 4.5dev2 or Godot 4.4.1



### System information

Godot v4.5.dev3 - macOS Sequoia (15.3.2) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M1 Max - Apple M1 Max (10 threads)

### Issue description

When using the Down cursor key to navigate focus in the GraphNode inspector (with focus_neighbor_bottom assigned), focus always lands on the node’s input/output “slots” (the little white rectangles or “ears”)—even when slot visibility is turned off—before it can move to the next GraphNode below. This both produces unwanted “ears” highlights and prevents a direct jump to the neighboring node.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/64a2648e-efc9-4a94-8b82-def5331e9c21)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/57658a89-f2fd-4560-b105-69a4348adef5)


### Steps to reproduce

	1.	Open Godot 4.5dev3 on macOS.
	2.	Create or open a scene containing two GraphNode instances, one positioned directly below the other.
	3.	In Project → Editor Settings → Interface → Focus:
	•	Assign Neighbor Bottom to the Down key.
	4.	(Optional) In the GraphNode’s Slots section, disable Show Slots.
	5.	Select the upper GraphNode in the 2D editor viewport.
	6.	Press the Down arrow key.

summary: Jolt Physics - SoftBody3D damping coefficient is wildly different than Godot Physics 3D

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6750 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.12033.1030) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

The results of the damping coefficient for SoftBody3D nodes while using the Jolt physics engine is wildly different than the results on the Godot Physics engine, and the value is clamped in the engine between 0 and 1 - making the softer flowing that was possible on the older physics engine impossible to replicate.

For Jolt Physics, even at a damping coefficient maxed out at 1.0 - the resulting simulation is very heavy. Jolt Physics' damping value of 1.0 (max) is roughly equivalent to the old physics' value at 0.01 - meaning we've lost almost all of the control over the stiffness and flow of cloth simulation using the SoftBody3D nodes.

Jolt:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c422b2f3-ca9b-4909-8733-6a5d8f6f588d

Godot:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e5eaa3af-4268-4407-8ee4-52678ca1ad5a

There is a difference between 0.01 and 1.0 for the Jolt demonstration, but the tightest setting is still more loose than the loosest setting previously.

### Steps to reproduce

MRP attached. Switch the physics engine between Jolt Physics and Godot Physics and run the test scene to see the stark difference between the soft body simulations.

This PR changes the scaling of multiple bezier animation keys to also scale the bezier handles along with it, which helps better preserve the shape of the curve as you scale it up/down.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fb14bcea-51b3-47ed-9080-3410d2ab8547

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d74676d2-ed0c-4823-ab15-9e549bb1694c

As you can hopefully see, the inherent shape of the selected curve is better preserved with this PR.

summary: OpenGL applying light from ViewportTexture to main Camera3D with different visibility layers

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.4.1 Stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Single-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Ti SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6603) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8700K CPU @ 3.70GHz (12 threads)

### Issue description

- Main Camera with Cull Mask layer 1
- Any light at layer 2
- SubViewport's Camera with default Cull Mask all layers active

Main Camera applying lights from ViewportTexture if it rendered on screen.
It works in editor too when you in Preview mode of Main Camera selecting SubViewport to see the texture in properties tab, or when making visible on screen Sprite3D or some shader with this VirtualTexture.

### Steps to reproduce

**Setup:**
1. Use/Make project in Compability or Mobile mode
2. Make Node3D scene
3. Add any MeshInstance3D for visualisation of light
4. Add Camera3D, move it to see mesh, set Cull Mask only layer 1 active
5. Add SubViewport
6. Create ViewportTexture resource and assign it to SubViewport
7. Add Camera3D to SubViewport, move it to see mesh too (may be different transform)
8. Add OmniLight3D, move it outside mesh to light it, set VisualInstance3D/Layers to only 2 layer active
9. Add Sprite3D, assing VirtualTexture to it, move it outside of mesh but not outside Main Camera range
10. Hide Sprite3D for now, it will be useful for **Check 2**

**Check 1:**
1. Select Main Camera and activate Preview mode at viewport of editor
2. Select SubViewport in NodeTree of Scene
Inspector tab showing ViewportTexture to you and applying it's light to main scene. If you have small editor widnow or expand some big property group like Rendering and scroll down, light will not be applied to main view due not rendering ViewportTexture.

**Check 2:**
1. Select Main Camera and activate Preview mode at viewport of editor
2. Show Sprite3D with VirtualTexture
When VirtualTexture rendered on screen, it's light applying to main view. You can also change Position or Rotation of Viewport Camera (for example drag X position with mouse) to see how light changing it's location

**Thoughts**
In my game project I could decide that I made something wrong with subviewport or camera settings, but if it applying not visible light from other camera only by just selecting SubViewport in editor, it really something very strange.
Also, I came to the conclusion that light comes from ViewportTexture at Check 2, when not wanted light showing on mesh, I tried to move Camera inside SubViewport, and light on mesh changed too.
Changing Culling Mask for two cameras not really helping.

summary: [3.x] load() duplicating contents of PackedScene

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested in v3.6.stable.official [de2f0f147]

### System information

Linux Mint 22.1 Xia Xfce - Godot 3.6 stable - Apple MacBook Pro (late 2012) - Intel Core i5-3210M

### Issue description

When using `load()` to open an `.scn` file as a `PackedScene` during runtime, I am coming across unexpected behaviour. The `PackedScene` can be instanced normally, but all the children of the `PackedScene` are duplicated. The duplicates have exactly the same names (I didn't think this was possible) but appear to have all their private variables set to default. For example, the duplicate `polygon` has an empty array for its `data` variable.

The method `get_node()` appears to return the duplicate child, instead of the original. This means I cannot access the original children without first freeing the duplicates.

I can't find any explanation of this online, so I think it is a bug.

The script of interest is simply:
```gdscript
extends Node2D

func _ready():
	$FileDialog.popup()


func _on_FileDialog_file_selected(path):
	var new = load(path).instance()
	add_child(new)
```

The `.scn` file has been generated in my game as part of a save-game system. This is the tree of the `.scn` file when opened as a scene in the editor.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/90576749-1a5a-43f6-8a41-61aa3d98bf2d)

This is the remote tree once the `.scn` file has been opened during runtime.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/db6b82af-ae91-4f8c-944e-8e308b5bc24b)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open project
2. Open `test_save.scn` as a scene and inspect the scene tree.
3. Run project (the main scene is `Node2D.tscn`)
4. Load `test_save.scn` through the file dialog.
5. Pause game.
6. Go to editor and look at the remote scene tree. 

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I was unable to replicate the bug when starting with a fresh project, so the MRP is based on a stripped-down version of my game. Most of the scripts and scenes are included in `test_save.scn`, so have been left in so it can be opened without throwing lots of errors. You can ignore these. The script of interest is `mrp.gd`, which is run in `Node2D.tscn`.

description.text:

### Issue description

We've had a lot of problem with WebGL ContextLost appearing on iPads and iPhones. It can take somewhere between a couple of seconds to several minutes for it to appear, but it made our app effectively unusable on these devices.
In our search for a cause, we noticed that we could reliably cause the error by _creating controls that use a StyleBoxFlat resource_, like buttons and panels.

I've created a small project where you can add different types of Controls to a GridContainer. Trying it on my iPad, I can trigger a ContextLost error by creating >30 buttons or panels, but only if they use a StyleBoxFlat in their styling. The other controls don't trigger this error.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Export the MRP to the web or [use this link](https://randofos.github.io/context_lost/)
2. Navigate to the page on an iPad or iPhone
3. Choose either Panel or Button as the object to create
4. Add objects to the grid until the error appears. On my iPad, the ContextLost error gets thrown after at least 30 objects have been created

summary: Support overriding `speaker_mode` in `AudioServer`

description.text:
**Updated**: _Refactored this to only handle setting/overriding the `speaker_mode` of a project. Channel remapping will be moved to another PR._

Adds support (via project settings under `audio/general/speaker_mode`) to the `AudioServer` to change the speaker mode (i.e. number of channels) that Godot uses internally, independent of the audio driver used. It can be used to test more channels (e.g. 7.1 surround when using a stereo audio setup), or fewer channels (e.g. stereo when using a 7.1 surround setup). This is intended to be used to help troubleshoot audio issues when testing with different speaker setups, or hard coding a speaker mode for a project.

~~This PR also adds the ability to remap internal channels onto different output channels of the audio device. For example, you can remap the rear or side stereo channels of a 7.1 surround system into the front channels of a stereo system in order to verify the audio is correct.~~

This is a extension of, and replacement for #105682. Like that PR, this feature makes it possible for Godot developers without the necessary surround sound peripheral to reproduce bugs and review fixes to issues that are the result of an unanticipated number of channels, such as: #92532

summary: AudioStreamInteractive treats AudioStreamRandomizer as if it has no length.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducable in v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6600 XT (RADV NAVI23) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

Normally, AudioStreamInteractive should wait for a stream to complete or reach the end of its beat count before progressing to the next clip.

If these clips are replaced with an AudioStreamRandomizer, they'll all play at once, as if they have zero length.

Here's the normal setup:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0032528d-386b-493b-b378-f1fb805469ae)

And the setup that causes this issue:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/72b95184-b8e3-4858-b369-77c32fdddf55)

Ideally, these two setups should produce the same result.

Of note: this works as expected with other streams such as AudioStreamPlaylist. Could be related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/104684

### Steps to reproduce

1) Create an AudioStreamPlayer
2) Give it an AudioStreamInteractive
3) Put at least two audio clips, with the first having "auto advance" enabled and set to go to the second clip.
4) If you put in two wav or ogg streams, it should behave as expected
5) Replace the clips with AudioStreamRandomizer streams, with the same wav/ogg stream inside it.
6) Play the stream and all streams will play at once.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mvp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19969265/mvp.zip)

summary: Editor documentation reports wrong parent class for some classes

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested on master branch v4.5.dev.custom_build [ce94b26de]
Tested on v4.4.1.stable.custom_build [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev (ce94b26de) - Manjaro Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sat, 29 Mar 2025 06:09:17 +0000 on Wayland - Wayland display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7900 XT (RADV NAVI31) - AMD Ryzen 9 7900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

When browsing the in-editor documentation for some of my custom classes, I noticed some of them were reporting the wrong parent class (`RefCounted` instead of the actual parent class, which does inherit from `RefCounted`).

In the images below, my `InSimRSTPacket` inherits a base `InSimPacket` class, but is shown as inheriting `RefCounted`. From the base `InSimPacket` class, only 4 classes are listed as inheriting from it, but there are actually close to 70 of them.
`InSimPacket` itself actually inherits from `LFSPacket`, which in turn inherits from `RefCounted`.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5da407b2-6bb7-4ba8-85d4-ca233c6d1939)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a11298c2-54f6-46bf-9aac-ebe32bad5bcd)

It may be worth noting that exporting the documentation via `godot --doctool --gdscript-docs` results in the same issue in the XML files.

### Steps to reproduce

Unknown to me at this point, I tried deleting the `.godot` directory, but the issue stayed exactly the same.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

See [this comment below](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/105926#issuecomment-2869657989) for a very small MRP.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.5-dev3, 4.4.1-stable, 4.3-stable, 4.2.2-stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev3 - macOS Sequoia (15.4.1) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 Max (Apple7) - Apple M1 Max (10 threads)

### Issue description

When running an exported project on an iOS device, the app crashes immediately when the screen is touched during the splash screen.

If the app is launched via the Run button in Xcode or using Godot’s Remote Deploy feature, the crash does not occur during the first run, even when touching the splash screen. However, if the app is closed and then reopened, it crashes upon touching the splash screen.

summary: Can´t see Groups at runtime in Remote

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.4.stable




### System information

Mac OS 15.3.2, M2 Pro,  Godot 4.4.stable, Compatibility mode

### Issue description


Basically the same issue as reported here in 2020: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/41073.

I am adding a group via code. Tried to verify via the remote tree if the group has been added. But the group icon is missing in the tree and also the node tab to the right of the inspector in unusable and has the following message: "Select a single node to edit its signals and groups.
I selected single nodes but nothing happens.

To me this is a bug and not expected behavior.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b5f0978e-2646-458c-9f51-1bf84a066354)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b2b3a630-3f57-4335-ab1e-3d85ab8ea454)

Best regards!

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a group via code in node tab right to the inspector tab or create via code "node.add_to_group(group_name)"
2. Run the scene
3. Go to Remote 
4. Check for groups in the tree

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Best regards!

summary: Can't Use Scene Undo on Wayland After Having Opened a Context Menu

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.5.dev.custom_build [f4c7a5ae1] and later
- Not reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable.arch_linux and v4.5.dev.custom_build [27b2ba667]

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev (84d3adcf2) - Arch Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sat, 26 Apr 2025 00:06:37 +0000 on Wayland - Wayland display driver, Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6600 XT (RADV NAVI23) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - sway version 1.10.1

### Issue description

Opening (then closing) a context menu at any point in the editor will prevent the user from undoing changes to the scene using the scene→undo (ctrl+z) tool, resulting in the output panel message "can't undo while mouse buttons are pressed." Using the history panel will still work however.

I believe it is a regression caused by pull #101774.

### Steps to reproduce

1. set editor setting `run/platforms/linuxbsd/prefer_wayland`
2. restart the editor
3. edit a project
4. make a change to a scene
5. right click to open a context menu
6. close the context menu
7. attempt to undo the changes using scene→undo (ctrl+z)
8. see the message "can't undo while mouse buttons are pressed" in the output panel

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
